eter , SharedParameter , SingletonParameter , SizeAttribute , StringParameter , TypeAttribute , WindowPropertiesAttribute public class Parameter extends Variable Parameter extends Variable with additional support for string-valued variables that mak

onal support for string-valued variables that makes these friendlier at the user interface level. In particular, this class supports an annotation that specifies choices for values. A user interface can use this to present a choice dialog that o

ce can use this to present a choice dialog that offers the specified values. This is typically used when a particular set of choices make sense. The values can be any expression, or if used in conjunction with string mode, any string. By defaul

njunction with string mode, any string. By default, an instance of Parameter, unlike Variable, is persistent. By convention, an instance of NamedObj has a set of attributes, some of which are visible to users and some of which are not. When a user

e to users and some of which are not. When a user interface presents these attributes for editing, it presents only those that are visible. By default, an instance of Parameter is visible, as indicated by the fact that it returns FULL in its getV

ated by the fact that it returns FULL in its getVisibility() method. This can be overridden by calling setVisibility(). Since: Ptolemy II 0.2 Version: $Id: Parameter.java,v 1.118.4.1 2007/02/04 01:37:31 cxh Exp $ Author: Neil Smyth, Edward A. Lee,

void exportMoML (java.io.Writer output, int depth, java.lang.String name) throws java.io.IOException Write a MoML description of this object, unless this object is not persistent. MoML

ect, unless this object is not persistent. MoML is an XML modeling markup language. In this class, the object is identified by the "property" element, with "name" and "class" (XML) attributes. The body of the element, between the "<property>"

The body of the element, between the "<property>" and "</property>", is written using the _exportMoMLContents() protected method, so that derived classes can override that method alone to alter only how the contents of this object are descri

only how the contents of this object are described. The text that is written is indented according to the specified depth, with each line (including the last one) terminated with a newline. Specified by: exportMoML in interface MoMLExportabl

pecified by: exportMoML in interface MoMLExportable Overrides: exportMoML in class NamedObj Parameters: output - The output stream to write to. depth - The depth in the hierarchy, to determine indenting. name - The name to use instead of the current
